summary: The live-ledger revalidation is correct and fail-closed. It resolves the authoritative ledger branch first, validates the returned commit SHA, and pins each Contents read to that immutable SHA before treating a 404 as conclusive absence. Unavailable ref/content reads and malformed content defer both terminal mutation paths with an alert, while schema-valid live provenance cancels the park. Issue-side admission preserves worker identity and exact issue binding, and the added direct plus mutation-boundary tests non-vacuously cover admission, verified absence, missing/malformed refs, unusable content, unavailable reads, binding mismatch, and alert-with-no-write behavior. I found no correctness, soundness, test-validity, injection, or security issue in the diff.
